name: agent-to-agent
description: Invoke another beige agent (or the same agent as a sub-agent) and hold a multi-turn conversation with it. Each call returns the target agent's full response plus a session key you can pass back to --session to continue the same conversation. Depth is enforced per-target. All cross-agent calls are opt-in — no targets are allowed until the targets config is set.
commands: --target <agent>  --session <key> --message-file <path> — Continue with a longer message from a file
--info                                      — Show which agents you may call and at what depth

targets.description: Map of target agent names to their config. Each key is an agent name that may be called. The special key "SELF" resolves to the calling agent's own name at runtime, enabling sub-agent patterns without hardcoding names. Per-agent restrictions are handled via beige's toolConfigs override mechanism. Absent = no calls permitted.

maxDepth.description: Default maximum nesting depth for targets that don't specify their own. 0 = no calls allowed. 1 (default) = agents may call agents, but those sub-agents may not invoke further agents. Individual targets can override this.
